mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating, equity discount rates stay suppressed and liquidity support dominates, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is flat or falling, or if the sign turns negative in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals income loss and weaker consumption, which pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events with UNRATE up more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in RSAFS after prior weakness indicates improving retail demand and near-term revenue momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive after a 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if positive turns in RSAFS after a two-month decline are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than chance, or if the mean 5-day return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO with HOUST also rising indicates production normalization and easing supply disruption, which should improve risk sentiment and raise S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in INDPRO and HOUST do not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if the combined signal has negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 contracts while PAYEMS declines, the economy is already in recessionary demand destruction, so S&P 500 returns should stay negative over the following quarter as earnings and risk appetite weaken together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts and PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if recessionary observations defined by falling GDPC1 and PAYEMS produce nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns on average, or if the hit rate for negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden cut in FEDFUNDS from an elevated level signals policy rescue after stress, and the discount-rate repricing should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls > 0.50pp within 3 months after being above 2%
failure_condition: Reject if rate-cut episodes from elevated FEDFUNDS levels do not lead to positive 20-day returns more often than not, or if the mean return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS continues to fall while UNRATE rises, labor income shock is broad-based rather than transitory, and S&P 500 should underperform over the next week as investors price a sharper demand hit.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 5-day return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: During a manufacturing recovery regime, rising INDPRO combined with higher HOUST indicates broad real-activity normalization and supply-chain repair, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if, within manufacturing recovery periods, the joint INDPRO-HOUST signal fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns on average or if its directional accuracy falls below 55%.
